symbols.title: Adjustment Lines
symbols.description: Lines indicating where to shorten or lengthen pattern pieces.

symbols.title: Buttonholes and Buttons
symbols.description: Marks showing placement of buttons and buttonholes on garments.

symbols.title: Centre Front / Centre Back
symbols.description: Dashed or dotted lines indicating the center front or back. Used to align pattern pieces correctly.

symbols.title: Cutting Line
symbols.description: Lines showing where to cut the fabric, often marked with a scissors icon.

symbols.title: Dart Lines
symbols.description: Lines and arrows indicating how to fold and stitch darts correctly.

symbols.title: Darts
symbols.description: Triangular shapes indicating where to stitch to create garment shaping.

symbols.title: Foldlines
symbols.description: Indicates the edge should be placed on the fabric fold. No seam allowance is added.

symbols.title: Grainlines
symbols.description: Arrows showing how to align pattern pieces parallel to the fabric selvedge.

symbols.title: Notches
symbols.description: Small marks on pattern edges used to match pieces accurately. Cut outward or snip inward slightly (about 3mm).

symbols.title: Shapes / Symbols
symbols.description: Dots, circles, and crosses marking key sewing points like alignment or stitch start/end.

symbols.title: Size Lines
symbols.description: Most patterns include multiple size lines. Use the designer’s size chart to choose your correct size, then cut along the corresponding line.

symbols.title: Stitching Lines
symbols.description: Dashed lines showing where stitching or topstitching should be applied.

symbols.title: Zipper Mark
symbols.description: Indicates where a zipper should be inserted.
